purpose: Clean isolated placement rules for 3D retrofit components before reconnecting to LGTNet room geometry.

zones.meaning: No plants, shelves, ladders, or trellis should block the active glazed/sliding-door path.
Wall-hosted planting can mount beside the opening on the interior wall face.
Wall-hosted planting can mount beside the opening on the interior wall face.
Large floor vegetation can sit inside the room if circulation remains clear.

component_rules.curtain: opening-mounted rail above the opening, many thin continuous fabric strips inside only
component_rules.planterShelf: wall-mounted back plate, shelf projects inward, pots sit on shelf
component_rules.hangingRail: wall/ceiling-adjacent rail, cords hang into room
component_rules.plantLadder: floor-standing ladder, back close to wall, shelves hold pots
component_rules.trellis: wall-mounted trellis frame beside opening, with denser irregularly scattered green sphere clusters across timber intersections; each sphere has a short connector back to the nearest timber rail
component_rules.floorPlants: large plants on floor zone, not in opening path
component_rules.slidingDoorFrame: connected head, sill, side jambs, and center mullion share one frame plane
component_rules.fullHeightDrape: opening-mounted full-height fabric drape on interior side
component_rules.shortSillCurtain: short fabric panels mounted near the opening head, ending above sill/floor path
component_rules.romanShade: compact fabric shade mounted at opening head with fold lines
component_rules.venetianBlind: thin horizontal slats inside opening frame
component_rules.verticalBlind: thin vertical slats from wall-bracketed track
component_rules.solarControlGlazing: changes existing glass colour/material only; no extra pane is added
component_rules.externalAwning: exterior-side awning above opening with bracket arms
component_rules.ceilingFan: ceiling support, hub, and blades centered on one shared axis
component_rules.rectangularPlanter: long rectangular pot for continuous vegetation, placed clear of opening path

plant_visual_types: topiary_or_ball: simple green ball connected by a visible stem
broadleaf: few clear stems from a crown with larger leaves
fern_spider_grass: radial slender leaves connected to crown
succulent: low rosette leaves connected to crown
trailing: vine chain with leaves connected along the vine
palm_tree: trunk with connected radial fronds
